collections.name: Core HPC
collections.summary: Starter skills for Slurm jobs, arrays, restartable workflows, notebooks, OpenMP placement, debugging, and storage triage.

collections.name: Data Movement
collections.summary: Skills for staging, transferring, validating, and managing research data on HPC storage.

collections.name: GPU, MPI, And Performance
collections.summary: Skills for validating GPU allocations, compiler/MPI compatibility, PyTorch DDP launches, MPI launches, OpenMP placement, and first-pass performance evidence.

collections.name: Software Stacks
collections.summary: Skills for debugging modules, compiler/MPI compatibility, Python and Conda environments, containers, and reproducible HPC software stacks.

collections.name: Workflow Engines
collections.summary: Skills for launching portable workflow engines on Slurm-backed HPC clusters.

site_adapters.name: Example Campus Cluster
site_adapters.summary: A non-production adapter showing how a site can map generic skills to local HPC policy.

skills.description: This skill provides templates for executing Apptainer or Singularity images under Slurm with explicit bind mounts, working directories, and optional GPU support.

examples.title: Run a container job under Slurm

skills.name: Apptainer Run Container
skills.summary: Run Apptainer containers safely on shared HPC systems.

skills.description: This skill helps users wrap long-running work with checkpoint directories, restart detection, progress state, and Slurm signal handling patterns that make reruns safer and less wasteful.
skills.name: Checkpoint Restart Workflow
skills.summary: Structure long HPC jobs so they can resume after time limits or preemption.

skills.description: This skill helps users create read-only checksum manifests for selected input or output files before transfer, archival, or support handoff.
skills.name: Checksum Manifest Create
skills.summary: Create checksum manifests for transfer validation and reproducibility.

skills.description: This skill gathers read-only evidence about loaded modules, compiler paths, MPI wrapper commands, and optional MPI smoke compilation so users can avoid mismatched compiler and MPI stacks.

examples.title: Collect compiler and MPI wrapper report

skills.name: Compiler MPI Matrix
skills.summary: Check compiler, MPI wrapper, and module compatibility before building HPC codes.

skills.description: This skill helps users create Conda/Mamba environments in explicit prefixes, manage package caches, export environment metadata, and avoid common HPC filesystem and module conflicts.
skills.name: Conda Mamba On HPC
skills.summary: Create Conda or Mamba environments while protecting shared HPC filesystems.

skills.description: This skill helps users prepare reproducible container definitions, choose site-approved build locations, record build metadata, and avoid expensive or policy-sensitive image builds on shared login filesystems.

examples.title: Record or run a container build plan

skills.name: Container Build For HPC
skills.summary: Plan and build Apptainer-compatible images for shared HPC systems.

skills.description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm staging wrapper that copies declared inputs to a job scratch directory, runs a workload there, and copies selected outputs back to durable storage.
skills.name: Dataset Staging To Scratch
skills.summary: Stage inputs to scratch, run work, and collect outputs from Slurm jobs.

skills.description: This skill provides a cautious EasyBuild workflow for searching easyconfigs, dry-running dependency resolution, and installing software into a user-controlled prefix.
skills.name: EasyBuild Install Software
skills.summary: Install scientific software with EasyBuild and generated modules.

skills.description: This skill provides a Globus CLI workflow for checking endpoints, submitting recursive transfers, and recording task ids for reliable HPC data movement.

examples.title: Submit and watch a Globus transfer

skills.name: Globus Transfer Dataset
skills.summary: Stage large datasets with Globus transfer and verification steps.

skills.description: This skill provides a Slurm GPU smoke test for NVIDIA or AMD GPU nodes, including device visibility, scheduler allocation variables, and optional Python framework checks.
skills.name: GPU Sanity Check
skills.summary: Verify GPU allocation, runtime visibility, and basic framework access.

skills.description: This skill provides conservative Slurm interactive session recipes for shell debugging, Jupyter, RStudio, and IDE tunnels.

examples.title: Start Jupyter on a compute node

skills.name: Interactive Session
skills.summary: Start short interactive compute sessions for debugging and notebooks.

skills.description: This skill provides a first-pass checklist for failed jobs, including out-of-memory events, time limits, missing modules, permission errors, and node failures.
skills.name: Job Failure Triage
skills.summary: Diagnose common HPC job failures from scheduler and log evidence.

skills.description: This skill provides a scheduler-backed Jupyter workflow with conservative resources, explicit port and token handling, and tunnel instructions that avoid running notebooks on login nodes.
skills.name: Jupyter On Slurm
skills.summary: Launch Jupyter notebooks inside short Slurm compute allocations.

skills.description: This skill helps users create a conservative archive plan with a file manifest, size summary, and optional tar.gz creation step for user-owned datasets.
skills.name: Large File Archive Prepare
skills.summary: Prepare large HPC datasets for archival, publication, or handoff.

skills.description: This skill gathers read-only environment evidence to debug broken software stacks, conflicting modules, missing libraries, and compiler or MPI mismatches.
skills.name: Module Environment Debug
skills.summary: Diagnose module, compiler, MPI, and library path conflicts.

skills.description: This skill provides a minimal MPI hello-world program and Slurm batch script to verify compiler, launcher, and node-to-node execution.
skills.name: MPI Hello And Benchmark
skills.summary: Compile and run MPI sanity checks across allocated nodes.

skills.description: This skill provides a Nextflow profile and launch wrapper for running portable workflows on Slurm-backed HPC clusters.
skills.name: Nextflow On Slurm
skills.summary: Configure Nextflow pipelines to run through the Slurm executor.

skills.description: This skill helps users request CPUs per task, set OpenMP environment variables, inspect thread placement hints, and avoid oversubscription in threaded HPC jobs.
skills.name: OpenMP Thread Affinity
skills.summary: Align OpenMP threads with Slurm CPU allocations and affinity settings.

skills.description: This skill wraps a command with basic timing, resource, I/O, and optional GPU telemetry so users can decide what to profile more deeply next.
skills.name: Performance Profile Basic
skills.summary: Collect first-pass performance evidence for an HPC workload.

skills.description: This skill helps users build user-owned Python virtual environments on HPC systems while recording module state, Python version, package inputs, and activation steps for reproducible jobs.
skills.name: Python Virtualenv On HPC
skills.summary: Create lightweight Python virtual environments with explicit HPC module assumptions.

skills.description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm smoke test for PyTorch DistributedDataParallel environments, including rank variables, GPU visibility, process group initialization, and a small all-reduce check.

examples.title: Submit a PyTorch DDP smoke test

skills.name: PyTorch DDP On Slurm
skills.summary: Launch and verify PyTorch distributed data parallel jobs on Slurm.

skills.description: This skill gives users and support teams a read-only checklist for storage-related failures such as full filesystems, quota limits, inode exhaustion, and path permission errors.
skills.name: Quota And Filesystem Triage
skills.summary: Diagnose quota, inode, and filesystem-space failures from user-visible evidence.

skills.description: This skill wraps a user command with metadata capture so support teams and collaborators can see command arguments, working directory, modules, environment, git state, optional input checksums, stdout, stderr, and exit code.
skills.name: Reproducible Run Capture
skills.summary: Capture command, environment, provenance, and logs for reproducible HPC runs.

skills.description: This skill helps users plan and run rsync transfers with conservative defaults, dry-run review, log capture, checksum-aware options, and a post-transfer validation checklist.

examples.title: Plan or run an rsync transfer

skills.name: Rsync Data Transfer
skills.summary: Transfer datasets with rsync dry-runs, resumable options, and validation hooks.

skills.description: This skill helps users inventory user-owned storage, identify large files and directories, and prepare a safe cleanup or staging plan before running storage-sensitive HPC workloads.
skills.name: Scratch Storage Management
skills.summary: Inspect scratch, project, and working-directory usage before HPC jobs.

skills.description: This skill helps users turn a manifest of independent inputs into a bounded Slurm job array with per-task logging, concurrency limits, and restart-friendly task selection.
skills.name: Slurm Job Array Patterns
skills.summary: Run parameter sweeps and many independent tasks with Slurm job arrays.

skills.description: This skill provides read-only commands for checking queued, running, completed, and failed Slurm jobs using squeue, sacct, scontrol, and log files.
skills.name: Slurm Monitor Job
skills.summary: Inspect Slurm job state, accounting records, and output paths.

skills.description: This skill uses sacct output to help users choose safer memory, CPU, and wall-time requests for similar Slurm jobs.
skills.name: Slurm Resource Estimator
skills.summary: Estimate future Slurm resource requests from accounting history.

skills.description: This skill helps users create Slurm sbatch scripts for CPU, GPU, MPI, and array workloads with conservative defaults and clear placeholders.
skills.name: Slurm Submit Job
skills.summary: Generate safe Slurm batch scripts for common HPC job shapes.

skills.description: This skill provides a starter Snakemake Slurm profile and launch wrapper for cluster execution with conservative default resources.
skills.name: Snakemake On Slurm
skills.summary: Configure Snakemake workflows to submit jobs through Slurm.

skills.description: This skill provides a starter spack.yaml and commands for concretizing, installing, and loading a user-owned scientific software environment.
skills.name: Spack Environment Create
skills.summary: Create reproducible Spack environments for HPC software stacks.
